rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are visible in the current AADL scope or properly imported via 'with' clauses.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the correct scalar types (int, real, bool) and ensuring all identifiers are properly resolved.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are explicitly visible in the current scope or declared within the same annex.
Use only named constants or visible features in guarantee expressions to avoid '<error>' type issues.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce property references or data types that are not declared or imported in the current AADL model.
Do not create guarantees with unresolved identifiers or mismatched types that result in '<error>' type errors.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
